concerns.analysis: Several meaningful images from the source PDF are missing or improperly extracted. The photo collage on the first page is missing (represented by an 'empty white rectangle'). The bar graphs and charts on pages 10 (Figures 1 and 2) and 17 (Figure 9) are missing and replaced with text placeholders. The charts on pages 15 (Figure 7) and 19 (Figure 11) were incorrectly extracted as crops of the page footers or blank backgrounds instead of the actual graphs.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The photo collage on page 1, bar graphs for Figure 1 and 2 on page 10, and the chart for Figure 7 on page 15.
concerns.output_quote: The images are absent, replaced with text placeholders like '[Image: A bar graph...]', or extracted as incorrect crops (e.g., alt='The image displays the footer of a document page.').

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 2 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=2. Examples: p8: "LAKE GASTON AQUATIC PLANT COMMUNITY - 2023"; p9: "LAKE GASTON HYDRILLA TUBER BANK ESTIMATES". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (38): Lake Gaston Aquatic Vegetation Report 2023; 1 | P a g e; Lake Gaston Aquatic Vegetation Report 2023
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=38, pages_affected=19/20. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 7 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (7 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p11: "...OW (Justicia americana)" showing Lake Gaston in 20"; p12: "...in 2023 showing the distribution of LYNGBYA (Micro"; p14: "...ed "HYDRILLA (Hydrilla verticillata) Lake Gaston -"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=7 (truncated=7, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

On page 1 of the source PDF, the phrases 'Prepared By:' and 'Submitted To:' are visibly underlined. In the extraction JSON, these elements are stored as standard paragraphs with markdown for italics, but the underline formatting is completely lost.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Prepared By: [and] Submitted To: (underlined in the PDF)

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 2 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=2. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
